pattern: A segment tree over rows — storing per-row remaining seats and prefix sums — lets gather and scatter queries find the right row in O(log n) time.
hints: For gather, you need the smallest-indexed row in [0, maxRow] that still has at least k contiguous seats — since seats are filled from left, 'remaining >= k' is the right condition.
For scatter, you need the total remaining capacity in [0, maxRow] to be >= k, then fill greedily row by row from the smallest row with any space.
A segment tree that stores the maximum remaining capacity in a range handles gather queries; a sum tree handles scatter's capacity check; both can be combined.

edge_inputs.description: basic gather then scatter sequence from the problem statement
gather when k equals m; fills entire row
scatter across multiple rows then gather finds next available row
maxRow constraint excludes rows with space; gather returns []
single row, m=1; gather succeeds then fails; scatter also fails
scatter with k larger than total remaining seats returns false
gather after scatter leaves partial row; gather in that row uses remaining seats

stress.description: random gather and scatter calls on a moderate-sized hall
stress.note: small hall so seats fill up quickly, exercising both success and failure paths

complexity.time: O(log n) per gather; O(n log n) worst-case scatter (naive walk)
